title: The Crystalline Archive Where Cold Grows Things

features.name: crystalline-formations
features.desc-on-touch: Geometries that grew in the cold. Each angle is a record: temperature, pressure, time. The archive keeps files here not by writing them, but by letting them freeze into shape.

features.name: frozen-pages
features.desc-on-touch: Paper held in ice, readable but untouchable. The text does not deteriorate here; it simply stops. Preservation tastes like silence.

features.name: icicle-choir
features.desc-on-touch: Hanging formations that hum at frequencies you almost hear. Each one is a different length, a different note. They have learned to sing preservation as a minor key.

features.name: the-frost-mirror
features.desc-on-touch: Its surface is too cold to reflect clearly, but you see yourself anyway—frozen, persistent, held in perfect recall. This is what remembering costs.

features.name: geometric-silence
features.desc-on-touch: The chamber's angles are perfect, mathematical, the result of cold enforcing order. No file here is ever lost. None of them are ever read again either.

inscription: preservation here means stopping: the archive learned that keeping something alive costs less than keeping it moving

file.name: The_Seventeen_Files_That_Chose_Stasis.mp3
file.note: Recordings of data frozen before corruption could reach it. The archive cannot tell if they are silent or simply paused.

exits.label: back toward the deepest chamber where preservation began
into caverns deeper still, where cold has learned to think
upward toward warmer chambers where the frozen files remember they were warm once
